segments.text: Jesus got into a boat, crossed over, and came to His own town.
Just then some men brought to Him a paralytic lying on a mat. When Jesus saw their faith, He said to the paralytic,
Take courage, son; your sins are forgiven.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: On seeing this, some of the scribes said to themselves,
This man is blaspheming!
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: But Jesus knew what they were thinking and said,
Why do you harbor evil in your hearts?
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Which is easier: to say, ‘Your sins are forgiven,’ or to say, ‘Get up and walk?’
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: But so that you may know that the Son of Man has authority on earth to forgive sins...
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Then He said to the paralytic,
Get up, pick up your mat, and go home.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: And the man got up and went home.
When the crowds saw this, they were filled with awe and glorified God, who had given such authority to men.
As Jesus went on from there, He saw a man named Matthew sitting at the tax booth.
Follow Me,
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: He told him, and Matthew got up and followed Him.
Later, as Jesus was dining at Matthew’s house, many tax collectors and sinners came and ate with Him and His disciples.
When the Pharisees saw this, they asked His disciples,
Why does your Teacher eat with tax collectors and sinners?
segments.speaker: Pharisees

segments.text: On hearing this, Jesus said,
It is not the healthy who need a doctor, but the sick.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: But go and learn what this means: ‘I desire mercy, not sacrifice.’ For I have not come to call the righteous, but sinners.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: At that time John’s disciples came to Jesus and asked,
Why is it that we and the Pharisees fast so often, but Your disciples do not fast?
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Jesus replied,

How can the guests of the bridegroom mourn while He is with them? But the time will come when the bridegroom will be taken from them; then they will fast.

## The Patches and the Wineskins
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: No one sews a patch of unshrunk cloth on an old garment. For the patch will pull away from the garment, and a worse tear will result.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Neither do men pour new wine into old wineskins. If they do, the skins will burst, the wine will spill, and the wineskins will be ruined. Instead, they pour new wine into new wineskins, and both are preserved.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: While Jesus was saying these things, a synagogue leader came and knelt before Him.
My daughter has just died,
segments.speaker: Leader

segments.text: he said.
But come and place Your hand on her, and she will live.
segments.speaker: Leader

segments.text: So Jesus got up and went with him, along with His disciples.
Suddenly a woman who had suffered from bleeding for twelve years came up behind Him and touched the fringe of His cloak.
She said to herself,
If only I touch His cloak, I will be healed.
segments.speaker: Woman

segments.text: Jesus turned and saw her.
Take courage, daughter,
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: He said,
your faith has healed you.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: And the woman was cured from that very hour.
When Jesus entered the house of the synagogue leader, He saw the flute players and the noisy crowd.
Go away,
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: He told them.
The girl is not dead, but asleep.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: And they laughed at Him.
After the crowd had been put outside, Jesus went in and took the girl by the hand, and she got up.
And the news about this spread throughout that region.

text: Jesus Heals the Blind and Mute

segments.text: As Jesus went on from there, two blind men followed Him, crying out,
Have mercy on us, Son of David!
segments.speaker: Men

segments.text: After Jesus had entered the house, the blind men came to Him.
Do you believe that I am able to do this?
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: He asked.
Yes, Lord,
segments.speaker: Men

segments.text: they answered.
Then He touched their eyes and said,
According to your faith will it be done to you.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: And their eyes were opened. Jesus warned them sternly,
See that no one finds out about this!
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: But they went out and spread the news about Him throughout the land.
As they were leaving, a demon-possessed man who was mute was brought to Jesus.
And when the demon had been driven out, the man began to speak. The crowds were amazed and said,
Nothing like this has ever been seen in Israel!
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: But the Pharisees said,
It is by the prince of demons that He drives out demons.
segments.speaker: The Pharisees

segments.text: Jesus went through all the towns and villages, teaching in their synagogues, preaching the gospel of the kingdom, and healing every disease and sickness.
When He saw the crowds, He was moved with compassion for them, because they were harassed and helpless, like sheep without a shepherd.
Then He said to His disciples,
The harvest is plentiful, but the workers are few.
segments.speaker: The Pharisees

segments.text: Ask the Lord of the harvest, therefore, to send out workers into His harvest.
segments.speaker: The Pharisees
